summary: Crystal‑clear Lamarre soundboard capture of the Oct ’84 show, highlighted by a soaring Bird Song, smoky Deal, tight Drumz>Space sequence and an emotional Baby Blue finale.
review: This 24‑bit Rob Lamarre recording delivers pristine sound that places you right in the venue while the performance shines throughout a classic early‑’80s first set and a near‑perfect second set. Highlights include an electrifying Bird Song, a blistering Deal, a tight Drumz>Space leading into The Wheel and Not Fade Away, and a heartfelt Baby Blue closing. Both reviewers praise the band’s interplay and the exceptional October ’84 sound.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviews are highly favorable (5★ and 4★) and emphasize superb musical moments plus excellent 24‑bit soundboard quality, justifying a rating above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: 24‑bit Rob Lamarre soundboard capture; crisp separation, balanced levels and no noticeable dropouts. Volume is well managed though a few brief sour guitar notes are noted.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A classic early‑80s first set builds into a strong second set that opens with Terrapin Station, flows through a tight PITB and China Doll, erupts in the Drumz>Space race to The Wheel > Not Fade Away, and ends on an emotional Baby Blue.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Spot‑on guitar tone and strong vocals on Terrapin Station; volume balanced though he hits a couple of sour notes.
band_member_comments.Phil: Profound, solid bass work that underpins the group's interplay throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland delivers relaxed yet edgy keyboard tones, listening attentively and complementing the band.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart provide a classic, tight Drumz>Space race to the finish, driving The Wheel > Not Fade Away with precision.
